channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Volunteer with the Sidewalk Warriors!
item.description: On Thursday, April 2, 2026, beginning at 4:30 PM, the CCPD and participating students and alumni will be volunteering with Sidewalk Warriors in Troy, New York. See below for information on how you can volunteer! Register in Handshake: https://rpi.joinhandshake.com/events/1890471/share_preview
item.author: Students

item.title: Smoothie Socials (RSVP)
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 10:00-11:00 AM. The Office of Alumni Engagement invites all students to join us on the first Wednesday of the month for RPI Smoothie Socials. Enjoy delicious smoothies and snacks, connect with fellow students and select staff, and get to know the Alumni Engagement team. Each month features a fun theme, and a unique giveaway for the first 50 students through the door! April’s theme: Welcome Whirl. April’s Giveaway: RPI-branded blender bottle. Register by March 30.
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Hosting Review Sessions Week of 3/22-3/29
item.description: Need some last minute review for your upcoming exam? Stop by for a Puck Cookie and a Review Session with our Success Center Tutors! Physics I: Sunday, 3/22 (3-5pm) at the Success Center Thermodynamics: Sunday, 3/22 (5-7pm) at the Success Center MATH 1520 Math Methods: Tuesday, 3/24 (5-7pm) Blitman Classroom in ECAV (2nd floor) Diff Eq.: Tuesday, 3/24 (5-7pm) at the Success Center
item.author: Students

item.title: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals 2027 Co-op Program Info Session
item.description: Regeneron is a leading biotechnology company that invents, develops, and commercializes life-transforming medicines for people with serious diseases.Virtual Info Session: March 25th - 7:00pm EST Explore Bold ideas. Go deep into your passion and learn from people at the top of their field. As a Regeneron Co-op, you’ll get hands-on, real-world experience in biotech.Our Co-oppositions are full-time from January to August (Spring-Summer), May/June-December (Summer-Fall), and August-December (Fall).
item.author: Students

item.title: 03/25/2026 Onboarding session for Qedma’s QESEM: error mitigation Qiskit function used with Quantum
item.description: RPI has recently acquired a license to Qedma’s QESEM: error mitigation Qiskit function for use with RPI’s dedicated IBM Quantum System One and Premium IBM Quantum Cloud account. An onboarding session has been scheduled March 25th, 9:30 AM in CII 3206. Please click in for additional details.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Orchestra and ESYO Symphony Orchestra Side-by-Side Concert on March 31
item.description: The combined forces of the RPI Orchestra and Empire State Youth Orchestra’s Symphony Orchestra unite to perform Gustav Mahler’s epic Symphony no. 1. The program will be led by RPI music faculty Dr. Robert Whalen, and will also include a performance of Edvard Grieg’s famous Concerto for Piano and Orchestra by 2025 Concerto Competition winner Armstrong Wang ’27. This performance will take place at 7pm on March 31st in the EMPAC Concert Hall. Admission is free.
item.author: Community

item.title: NROTC to Host Helicopter Landing on ’86 Field
item.description: On Wednesday April 1 at 11:30 AM, RPI’s Naval Reserve Officers’ Training Corps (NROTC) will host the landing and display of a U.S. Marine Corps Bell UH-1Y Venom helicopter on ’86 Field. Members of the campus community are invited to view the helicopter landing at 12 pm and meet the pilots until 2 pm. The community is also invited to observe take off between 3:30 - 4 pm.
item.author: Community

item.title: The Future of Computing Research Collaboration (FCRC) 2026 Annual Workshop
item.description: This full-day workshop brings together RPI and IBM leadership, faculty, researchers, and students to highlight strategic priorities, research progress across the four FCRC tracks (AI Algorithms, AI HW/SW Codesign, Semiconductor Technology, and Quantum Computing), and future collaboration opportunities. Visit https://fcrc.rpi.edu/news for the agenda and registration link.
item.author: Community

item.title: Department of Economics Seminar: Olga Schurchkov, Wellesley College
item.description: Please join us as the Department of Economics welcomes Dr. Olga Shurchkov, who will present: “The Optimal Size of a Lie: Persuasion and Perceptions in Cheating Games” on Wednesday, March 25 at 3:30 PM in SAGE 3713.
item.author: Community

item.title: Sports Rules and Musicals: A Conversation with Artist Ericka Beckman
item.description: Engaging live-action choreographed sequences, as well as handcrafted layered animation, and face paced soundtrack, Beckman transforms the cultural norms communicated through common-place material such as fairytales and games. As part of the Department of Games and Experiential Media’s “Gamefest” programming and Arts’ iEar Presents, Beckman will give a talk on her work and the relationship between her art and games from 12pm-2pm on March 28 at EMPAC Theater.
item.author: Community

item.title: Fair Game Films: A Selected Screening of the Films of Ericka Beckman
item.description: As a part of the Department of Games and Experiential Media’s “Gamefest” and Arts’ iEar Presents, this screening and discussion will draw on Beckman’s longstanding exploration of how physical action, and our memory of it, constructs cultural images and influences both our behavior and perception of reality. Visit EMPAC Theater at 2:15pm on March 28.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Community Easter Dinner
item.description: Date: Sunday, April 5, 2026 6:30 PM - 9:30 PM - This Dinner is a yearly event hosted by Rensselaer Christian Association, Cru RPI, and Rensselaer Newman to serve the RPI community. We invite all of you to come enjoy a good meal and fellowship. If you are planning to attend, we ask that you RSVP using the form below. This will allow us to make sure we have enough food for everyone. https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSc-4w8TCGuyrUUZrIVWBXnG0Ws7ScX8fNpxbIryK5BSxkr5qw/viewform
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Workshop: RPI MAX for Staff: Leveraging Feedback and Career Opportunities
item.description: Date: Thursday, March 26, 2026 8:00 AM - 9:30 AM - As an employee, you work in partnership with your manager to align expectations and set meaningful goals. Throughout the year, you check in together, recalibrate as needed, and celebrate progress and achievements. This workshop focuses on supporting your individual career development, preparing for the annual manager evaluation, setting effective goals, and using tools that can help you advance your career.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Workshop: RPI MAX for Supervisors: Performance and Coaching
item.description:
Date: Thursday, March 26, 2026 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM - As a manager, you play a central role in guiding the performance process for your employees. This workshop introduces the core principles of performance coaching, including how to identify and evaluate performance gaps, set effective goals, and lead the RPI MAX performance process. Participants will also explore practical tools for building strong, high‑performing teams.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
